title: Review: wallet round 17
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — Seven correctness bugs including two P0s (infinite loop, non-atomic insert), plus five P1 maintainer-burden issues covering fragile caller contracts (anchor_height, params=None, zero-change fee coupling), destructive cache corruption on download failure, and silent DB downgrade. Any one of the P0s can produce data loss or hang in production.
Confidence: 25% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) I did not trace the full fee-rejection path through zcash_primitives Builder::build to confirm exactly when FeeRule::standard() rejects mismatched fees — there may be a window where a wrong fee produces a signed but invalid transaction rather than an error; (2) I did not verify the interaction between FIFO note ordering in select_notes_spendable and any tiebreaking behavior when block_height values are equal (same block, multiple notes); (3) I did not audit client.rs gRPC retry semantics under partial failure. Would raise confidence if: a test exercises build_shielded_tx with a zero-change transaction end-to-end against a mock builder that validates the fee; and if select_with_fee has a property test covering convergence with adversarially-chosen note value sets.
